mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above its 10-year median.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative risk sentiment and downward pressure on S&P 500 prices within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their respective 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint exceedance of 12-month moving averages yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive equity returns during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is unchanged, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which rapidly compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >65% of observations when CPIAUCSL YoY growth surpasses 4.0%, invalidating the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, which boosts household income and corporate profit margins, yielding positive quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k and UNRATE declines > 0.1pp simultaneously
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings fundamentals, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals supply chain bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, which triggers immediate negative market repricing over the next five trading days during periods of economic uncertainty.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of instances following a 0.5% monthly decline in INDPRO, or the signal loses predictive power when VIX exceeds 25.
